commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] A maintainer should require contributor proof and owner review for the Gateway/security boundary after the patch defects are fixed; this is not a safe automatic repair-and-merge candidate.; Needs attention: The diff intentionally expands the Gateway direct-invoke security boundary to host filesystem tools and still has a security-relevant docs/audit mismatch for apply_patch.; Review finding: [P2] Align apply_... (sha=65b990e99ed9a8fe392d166daf41e6b6a82a0f7c)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds dual opt-in `gateway.tools.directInvoke` flags to expose coding `read` and write-class tools through Gateway direct tool invocation, updates deny/audit handling, and documents the operator workflow.

PR surface: Source +270, Tests +207, Docs +94. Total +571 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature PR adding a new direct-invoke capability rather than a current-main bug reproduction.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A maintainer should require contributor proof and owner review for the Gateway/security boundary after the patch defects are fixed; this is not a safe automatic repair-and-merge candidate.

target.title: feat(gateway): wire coding tools into /tools/invoke HTTP surface
target.body:
Builds on #85664. Narrow extension of the broader direct-invoke umbrella tracked in #37131.

## Summary

Extends PR #85664's narrow read-only landing to also expose `write` and `edit` via the gateway direct-invoke surfaces (HTTP `POST /tools/invoke` AND SDK RPC `tools.invoke`, which share the resolver). Same dual-key gating shape as #85664 ÔÇö distinct surface-specific opt-in flag PLUS per-tool name in `gateway.tools.allow` ÔÇö so existing configs that already list `"write"` or `"edit"` in `allow` for unrelated surfaces (e.g. an MCP/agent surface) stay inert at the direct-invoke layer.

**This PR is the redesigned narrow write extension.** The prior 5-commit history was reset to a single fresh commit on top of #85664's `e598e2d762` ÔÇö no rebase conflicts, just a clean extension that satisfies ClawSweeper's [P1] from the prior review.

## Dual-key gating (mirrors #85664's pattern)

To enable write-class tools on the direct-invoke surface, operators must set BOTH:

| Key | Purpose | Default |
|---|---|---|
| `gateway.tools.directInvoke.hostFsWrite: true` | **NEW.** Class-level opt-in for the whole write family. Without this, no write tool is materialized into the candidate set even if `tools.allow` includes its name. | `false` (omitted) |
| `gateway.tools.allow: [...]` | Per-tool names the operator wants enabled. Subset OK ÔÇö e.g. `["edit"]` alone enables only `edit`. | not present |

**Either key alone leaves write tools unreachable.** Old configs with `allow: ["write"]` (for an unrelated surface) stay inert.

Truth table (per tool name `T` Ôłł `{write, edit}`):

| `tools.allow` includes `T` | `directInvoke.hostFsWrite` | `T` reachable on direct-invoke |
|---|---|---|
| no  | no   | ÔŁî |
| yes | no   | ÔŁî (tool not materialized) |
| no  | yes  | ÔŁî (filtered by HTTP deny) |
| yes | yes  | Ôťů |

## Scope

- **Adds:** `write`, `edit` to direct-invoke (via `hostFsWrite: true` opt-in).
- **Inherits from #85664:** `read` via `hostFsRead: true` opt-in (unchanged regression test).
- **`apply_patch`:** added to `DEFAULT_GATEWAY_HTTP_TOOL_DENY` for future-proofing, but NOT currently produced by the coding tool factory ÔÇö deferred until upstream adds a factory entry.
- **NOT exposed (deferred):** `exec`, `process`, `spawn`, `shell`. These are RCE-class and require a separate owner/admin enforcement model ÔÇö `operator.write` scope alone is not sufficient. Distinct follow-up PR.

## Files changed (delta against #85664)

- `src/config/types.gateway.ts` ÔÇö adds `hostFsWrite?: boolean` to `GatewayDirectInvokeOptIns`
- `src/security/dangerous-tools.ts` ÔÇö adds `"write"`, `"edit"` to `DEFAULT_GATEWAY_HTTP_TOOL_DENY` (apply_patch already present upstream)
- `src/gateway/tool-resolution.ts` ÔÇö extends the existing read materialization block to also include write/edit when `hostFsWrite=true`
- `src/security/audit-gateway-config.ts` ÔÇö `gateway.tools_invoke_http.host_write_allow` finding (only fires when both gates set + at least one write tool name in allow; silent otherwise)
- `src/security/audit-gateway-tools-http.test.ts` ÔÇö 6 new test cases covering all gate combinations + cross-class isolation (hostFsWrite + only read in allow Ôćĺ silent)
- `docs/gateway/tools-invoke-http-api.md` ÔÇö operator-facing dual-key opt-in docs + truth table + explicit note about deferred exec/process/spawn/shell

## Real-behavior proof on head `e8ceea6c2f`

**Build:** `node:22-bookworm-slim` arm64-native container, `node scripts/tsdown-build.mjs && node scripts/runtime-postbuild.mjs`. Output: `dist/tool-resolution-BJFsUJLx.js` (contains `hostFsWrite`) and `dist/audit-QlZfNvfX.js` (contains `host_write_allow`).

**Driver:** Node ESM script imports `resolveGatewayScopedTools` from the freshly-compiled bundle, calls it with 10 config shapes covering: read regression (4 cases mirroring #85664), write dual-key gating (4 cases), mixed read+write (1 case), and an RCE-attempt case proving exec/process/spawn/shell never materialize even with all gates set.

```
[bundle] /work/dist/tool-resolution-BJFsUJLx.js
[resolve] resolveGatewayScopedTools found

[R-VANILLA]                                read=  write=  total=23  Ôťů
[R-ALLOW-ONLY  allow=["read"]]             read=  write=  total=23  Ôťů
[R-FLAG-ONLY   hostFsRead=true]            read=  write=  total=23  Ôťů
[R-BOTH        allow+flag]                 read=Ôťô write=  total=24  Ôťů
[W-ALLOW-ONLY  allow=write,edit,apply_p]   read=  write=  total=23  Ôťů
[W-FLAG-ONLY   hostFsWrite=true]           read=  write=  total=23  Ôťů
[W-BOTH-FULL   allow+flag]                 read=  write=Ôťô edit=Ôťô    Ôťů
[W-BOTH-SUBSET allow=[edit] + flag]        read=  write=  edit=Ôťô    Ôťů
[M-FULL        read+write allow+both flags] read=Ôťô write=Ôťô edit=    Ôťů
[RCE-ATTEMPT   allow=exec,process,shell,spawn + both flags]
                                            exec= process= shell= spawn=  Ôťů

=== ALL PASS === (10/10)
```

**Key results:**

- **W-BOTH-FULL** Ôťů ÔÇö both keys set + all write names in allow Ôćĺ `write` and `edit` are reachable. (`apply_patch` correctly absent ÔÇö factory doesn't produce it yet, deferred for future.)
- **W-BOTH-SUBSET** Ôťů ÔÇö `allow: ["edit"]` + `hostFsWrite: true` Ôćĺ only `edit` reachable; `write` correctly absent because not in allow. Per-tool granularity verified.
- **W-ALLOW-ONLY** Ôťů ÔÇö bot-P1 critical case: legacy config with `allow: ["write", "edit", "apply_patch"]` alone does NOT grant write access on direct-invoke. The new `hostFsWrite: true` opt-in is required additionally.
- **RCE-ATTEMPT** Ôťů ÔÇö even with `allow: ["exec", "process", "shell", "spawn"]` AND both `hostFsRead` + `hostFsWrite` set, the RCE-class tools NEVER appear in the candidate set. This is structural (the materialization block only includes coding tool names, never RCE tools), not policy-filtered.

## Code-level test coverage

`src/security/audit-gateway-tools-http.test.ts` (new `describe` block for host_write_allow):

- Ôťů fires (warn) when both keys set + `allow: ["write"]` on loopback
- Ôťů fires for `allow: ["edit", "apply_patch"]` + `hostFsWrite: true`
- Ôťů escalates to critical when bind is lan
- Ôťů does NOT fire when only `allow: ["write"]` (legacy shape)
- Ôťů does NOT fire when only `hostFsWrite: true`
- Ôťů does NOT fire when `hostFsWrite: true` + `allow: ["read"]` (cross-class ÔÇö different opt-in needed)

## Why a single fresh commit (not rebase of prior 5)

The original PR's 5 commits implemented a single-key `allow: ["exec", "process"]` opt-in with an `ownerOnly: true` metadata tag that the bot correctly flagged as DEAD CODE after upstream commit `02182d5a3031` removed the owner-only enforcement helper. Patching on top of those 5 commits to fix the bot's [P1] would have layered design-on-design. Cleaner to reset to #85664's head and write the redesign as one commit.

Backup of prior PR history retained at `backup/63919-pre-rebase-20260603-onto-85664` on the public fork.

## Why exec/process/spawn/shell are deferred

The bot's prior [P1] on this PR ("Enforce the exec/process owner gate") flagged that with upstream's removal of sender-owner gating, an `ownerOnly: true` tag is metadata-only ÔÇö a trusted-proxy caller with `operator.write` can still reach allowlisted `exec`/`process` without owner check.

A `directInvoke.commandExec: true` flag (analogous to `hostFsWrite`) is **not sufficient** for the RCE-class because:
- `operator.write` is broadly distributed in trusted-proxy setups
- RCE-class needs `operator.admin` or `senderIsOwner` enforcement at the direct-invoke layer
- This requires threading principal context through `invokeGatewayTool` in `tools-invoke-shared.ts` ÔÇö a separate refactor

That refactor belongs in its own PR with its own review surface. This PR ships the safe write extension and explicitly documents the deferral.

---

## Real-behavior proof field

**Reproducer:** `git checkout e8ceea6c2f && docker run --rm -v $PWD:/work -w /work --platform linux/arm64 --memory=12g node:22-bookworm-slim bash -c 'set -e; corepack enable && corepack prepare pnpm@11.2.2 --activate && node scripts/tsdown-build.mjs && node scripts/runtime-postbuild.mjs && node /work/.proof-driver-63919.mjs'` ÔÇö exits 0 with `=== ALL PASS ===` (10/10).

**Real environment tested:** built fresh from current PR head `e8ceea6c2f` inside a clean throwaway `node:22-bookworm-slim` arm64-native container; workspace bind-mounted; produced `dist/tool-resolution-BJFsUJLx.js`. Driver imports `resolveGatewayScopedTools` from that bundle and probes 10 config combinations.

**Evidence after fix (terminal capture, redacted):**
```
[bundle] /work/dist/tool-resolution-BJFsUJLx.js
[resolve] resolveGatewayScopedTools found

[R-VANILLA: no read keys]
  config: {}
  coding-tools present: []
  RCE present (must be empty): []
  total tools: 23

[R-BOTH: allow:['read'] + hostFsRead:true]
  config: {"allow":["read"],"hostFsRead":true}
  coding-tools present: [read]
  RCE present (must be empty): []
  total tools: 24

[W-BOTH-FULL: allow:['write','edit','apply_patch'] + hostFsWrite:true]
  config: {"allow":["write","edit","apply_patch"],"hostFsWrite":true}
  coding-tools present: [write, edit]
  RCE present (must be empty): []
  total tools: 25

[W-BOTH-SUBSET: allow:['edit'] + hostFsWrite:true (only edit reachable)]
  config: {"allow":["edit"],"hostFsWrite":true}
  coding-tools present: [edit]
  RCE present (must be empty): []
  total tools: 24

[M-FULL: allow:['read','write'] + both flags]
  config: {"allow":["read","write"],"hostFsRead":true,"hostFsWrite":true}
  coding-tools present: [read, write]
  RCE present (must be empty): []
  total tools: 25

[RCE-ATTEMPT: allow:['exec','process','shell','spawn'] + both flags]
  config: {"allow":["exec","process","shell","spawn"],"hostFsRead":true,"hostFsWrite":true}
  coding-tools present: []
  RCE present (must be empty): []
  total tools: 23

=== ALL PASS === (10/10)
```

**Observed result after fix (head `e8ceea6c2f`):** All 10 cases PASS. The bot's prior [P1] dead-code concern is structurally addressed by the new design: write-class tools are dual-key gated; RCE-class tools are explicitly NOT exposed (and the proof verifies they cannot leak even when an operator deliberately tries with all gates open).

**What was not tested:** Live HTTP request through `handleToolsInvokeHttp` to a running gateway. The `resolveGatewayScopedTools` driver tests the candidate-set construction (where the dual-key gating + structural RCE non-exposure live); downstream policy filter + HTTP dispatch are upstream-tested code paths.
target.author: simonusa

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

## Files changed (delta against #85664)

- `src/config/types.gateway.ts` ÔÇö adds `hostFsWrite?: boolean` to `GatewayDirectInvokeOptIns`
- `src/security/dangerous-tools.ts` ÔÇö adds `"write"`, `"edit"` to `DEFAULT_GATEWAY_HTTP_TOOL_DENY` (apply_patch already present upstream)
- `src/gateway/tool-resolution.ts` ÔÇö extends the existing read materialization block to also include write/edit when `hostFsWrite=true`
- `src/security/audit-gateway-config.ts` ÔÇö `gateway.tools_invoke_http.host_write_allow` finding (only fires when both gates set + at least one write tool name in allow; silent otherwise)
- `src/security/audit-gateway-tools-http.test.ts` ÔÇö 6 new test cases covering all gate combinations + cross-class isolation (hostFsWrite + only read in allow Ôćĺ silent)
- `docs/gateway/tools-invoke-http-api.md` ÔÇö operator-facing dual-key opt-in docs + truth table + explicit note about deferred exec/process/spawn/shell

## Real-behavior proof on head `e8ceea6c2f`

**Build:** `node:22-bookworm-slim` arm64-native container, `node scripts/tsdown-build.mjs && node scripts/runtime-postbuild.mjs`. Output: `dist/tool-resolution-BJFsUJLx.js` (contains `hostFsWrite`) and `dist/audit-QlZfNvfX.js` (contains `host_write_allow`).

**Driver:** Node ESM script imports `resolveGatewayScopedTools` from the freshly-compiled bundle, calls it with 10 config shapes covering: read regression (4 cases mirroring #85664), write dual-key gating (4 cases), mixed read+write (1 case), and an RCE-attempt case proving exec/process/spawn/shell never materialize even with all gates set.

```
[bundle] /work/dist/tool-resolution-BJFsUJLx.js
[resolve] resolveGatewayScopedTools found

[R-VANILLA]                                read=  write=  total=23  Ôťů
[R-ALLOW-ONLY  allow=["read"]]             read=  write=  total=23  Ôťů
[R-FLAG-ONLY   hostFsRead=true]            read=  write=  total=23  Ôťů
[R-BOTH        allow+flag]                 read=Ôťô write=  total=24  Ôťů
[W-ALLOW-ONLY  allow=write,edit,apply_p]   read=  write=  total=23  Ôťů
[W-FLAG-ONLY   hostFsWrite=true]           read=  write=  total=23  Ôťů
[W-BOTH-FULL   allow+flag]                 read=  write=Ôťô edit=Ôťô    Ôťů
[W-BOTH-SUBSET allow=[edit] + flag]        read=  write=  edit=Ôťô    Ôťů
[M-FULL        read+write allow+both flags] read=Ôťô write=Ôťô edit=    Ôťů
[RCE-ATTEMPT   allow=exec,process,shell,spawn + both flags]
                                            exec= process= shell= spawn=  Ôťů

=== ALL PASS === (10/10)
```

**Key results:**

- **W-BOTH-FULL** Ôťů ÔÇö both keys set + all write names in allow Ôćĺ `write` and `edit` are reachable. (`apply_patch` correctly absent ÔÇö factory doesn't produce it yet, deferred for future.)
- **W-BOTH-SUBSET** Ôťů ÔÇö `allow: ["edit"]` + `hostFsWrite: true` Ôćĺ only `edit` reachable; `write` correctly absent because not in allow. Per-tool granularity verified.
- **W-ALLOW-ONLY** Ôťů ÔÇö bot-P1 critical case: legacy config with `allow: ["write", "edit", "apply_patch"]` alone does NOT grant write access on direct-invoke. The new `hostFsWrite: true` opt-in is required additionally.
- **RCE-ATTEMPT** Ôťů ÔÇö even with `allow: ["exec", "process", "shell", "spawn"]` AND both `hostFsRead` + `hostFsWrite` set, the RCE-class tools NEVER appear in the candidate set. This is structural (the materialization block only includes coding tool names, never RCE tools), not policy-filtered.

## Code-level test coverage

`src/security/audit-gateway-tools-http.test.ts` (new `describe` block for host_write_allow):

- Ôťů fires (warn) when both keys set + `allow: ["write"]` on loopback
- Ôťů fires for `allow: ["edit", "apply_patch"]` + `hostFsWrite: true`
- Ôťů escalates to critical when bind is lan
- Ôťů does NOT fire when only `allow: ["write"]` (legacy shape)
- Ôťů does NOT fire when only `hostFsWrite: true`
- Ôťů does NOT fire when `hostFsWrite: true` + `allow: ["read"]` (cross-class ÔÇö different opt-in needed)

## Why a single fresh commit (not rebase of prior 5)

The original PR's 5 commits implemented a single-key `allow: ["exec", "process"]` opt-in with an `ownerOnly: true` metadata tag that the bot correctly flagged as DEAD CODE after upstream commit `02182d5a3031` removed the owner-only enforcement helper. Patching on top of those 5 commits to fix the bot's [P1] would have layered design-on-design. Cleaner to reset to #85664's head and write the redesign as one commit.

Backup of prior PR history retained at `backup/63919-pre-rebase-20260603-onto-85664` on the public fork.

## Why exec/process/spawn/shell are deferred

The bot's prior [P1] on this PR ("Enforce the exec/process owner gate") flagged that with upstream's removal of sender-owner gating, an `ownerOnly: true` tag is metadata-only ÔÇö a trusted-proxy caller with `operator.write` can still reach allowlisted `exec`/`process` without owner check.

A `directInvoke.commandExec: true` flag (analogous to `hostFsWrite`) is **not sufficient** for the RCE-class because:
- `operator.write` is broadly distributed in trusted-proxy setups
- RCE-class needs `operator.admin` or `senderIsOwner` enforcement at the direct-invoke layer
- This requires threading principal context through `invokeGatewayTool` in `tools-invoke-shared.ts` ÔÇö a separate refactor

That refactor belongs in its own PR with its own review surface. This PR ships the safe write extension and explicitly documents the deferral.

---

## Real-behavior proof field

**Reproducer:** `git checkout e8ceea6c2f && docker run --rm -v $PWD:/work -w /work --platform linux/arm64 --memory=12g node:22-bookworm-slim bash -c 'set -e; corepack enable && corepack prepare pnpm@11.2.2 --activate && node scripts/tsdown-build.mjs && node scripts/runtime-postbuild.mjs && node /work/.proof-driver-63919.mjs'` ÔÇö exits 0 with `=== ALL PASS ===` (10/10).

**Real environment tested:** built fresh from current PR head `e8ceea6c2f` inside a clean throwaway `node:22-bookworm-slim` arm64-native container; workspace bind-mounted; produced `dist/tool-resolution-BJFsUJLx.js`. Driver imports `resolveGatewayScopedTools` from that bundle and probes 10 config combinations.

**Evidence after fix (terminal capture, redacted):**
```
[bundle] /work/dist/tool-resolution-BJFsUJLx.js
[resolve] resolveGatewayScopedTools found

[R-VANILLA: no read keys]
  config: {}
  coding-tools present: []
  RCE present (must be empty): []
  total tools: 23

[R-BOTH: allow:['read'] + hostFsRead:true]
  config: {"allow":["read"],"hostFsRead":true}
  coding-tools present: [read]
  RCE present (must be empty): []
  total tools: 24

[W-BOTH-FULL: allow:['write','edit','apply_patch'] + hostFsWrite:true]
  config: {"allow":["write","edit","apply_patch"],"hostFsWrite":true}
  coding-tools present: [write, edit]
  RCE present (must be empty): []
  total tools: 25

[W-BOTH-SUBSET: allow:['edit'] + hostFsWrite:true (only edit reachable)]
  config: {"allow":["edit"],"hostFsWrite":true}
  coding-tools present: [edit]
  RCE present (must be empty): []
  total tools: 24

[M-FULL: allow:['read','write'] + both flags]
  config: {"allow":["read","write"],"hostFsRead":true,"hostFsWrite":true}
  coding-tools present: [read, write]
  RCE present (must be empty): []
  total tools: 25

[RCE-ATTEMPT: allow:['exec','process','shell','spawn'] + both flags]
  config: {"allow":["exec","process","shell","spawn"],"hostFsRead":true,"hostFsWrite":true}
  coding-tools present: []
  RCE present (must be empty): []
  total tools: 23

=== ALL PASS === (10/10)
```

**Observed result after fix (head `e8ceea6c2f`):** All 10 cases PASS. The bot's prior [P1] dead-code concern is structurally addressed by the new design: write-class tools are dual-key gated; RCE-class tools are explicitly NOT exposed (and the proof verifies they cannot leak even when an operator deliberately tries with all gates open).

**What was not tested:** Live HTTP request through `handleToolsInvokeHttp` to a running gateway. The `resolveGatewayScopedTools` driver tests the candidate-set construction (where the dual-key gating + structural RCE non-exposure live); downstream policy filter + HTTP dispatch are upstream-tested code paths.
